title: Ebola Outbreak in Congo Spreads to New Areas as Death Toll Rises
subtitle: Health experts warn of the fastest-growing Ebola crisis ever recorded as conflict and funding cuts hinder response efforts.

summary.en: The Democratic Republic of the Congo is facing its fastest-growing Ebola outbreak in history. Official data shows 1,759 confirmed cases and 600 deaths across several provinces. The virus has also spread to neighboring Uganda, causing further concern for health officials. Ongoing armed conflicts and a lack of international funding are making it difficult to control the disease. Researchers have recently begun clinical trials to test potential treatments for this rare virus.
summary.zh: 剛果民主共和國正面臨史上擴散速度最快的伊波拉疫情。官方數據顯示，多個省份共計有一千七百五十九例確診與六百人死亡。病毒亦已擴散至鄰國烏干達，引發衛生官員進一步擔憂。持續的武裝衝突與國際資金短缺，使得控制疫情變得困難重重。研究人員近期已展開臨床試驗，以測試針對此種罕見病毒的潛在療法。

paragraphs.en: The Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) is currently facing a serious public health emergency as a deadly Ebola outbreak continues to spread. According to government data released on July 8, the country has recorded 1,759 confirmed cases and 600 deaths. This outbreak, which began in May, is now moving beyond its original center in Ituri province, reaching new areas including Tshopo and Haut-Uélé. The virus has also crossed national borders, with 20 confirmed cases and two deaths reported in neighboring Uganda.
paragraphs.zh: 剛果民主共和國（DRC）目前正面臨嚴峻的公共衛生緊急狀態，致命的伊波拉病毒疫情持續蔓延。根據七月八日公布的政府數據，該國已記錄到一千七百五十九例確診病例，並造成六百人死亡。這波始於五月的疫情，目前已超出最初的伊圖里省（Ituri）中心地帶，擴散至包括喬波省（Tshopo）與上韋萊省（Haut-Uélé）在內的新區域。病毒也已跨越國界，鄰國烏干達亦通報了二十例確診與兩起死亡案例。

paragraphs.en: Experts are deeply concerned about the speed of this crisis. Wessam Mankoula, the head of emergency preparedness for the Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention, recently told reporters that this is the fastest-growing Ebola outbreak ever recorded. The disease is caused by the rare Bundibugyo variant. Unlike other types of Ebola, this specific version has no approved vaccine or treatment, which makes the situation much more dangerous for local communities.
paragraphs.zh: 專家們對這場危機的擴散速度深感憂心。非洲疾病管制暨預防中心（Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention）緊急應變負責人曼庫拉（Wessam Mankoula）近日向記者表示，這是史上擴散速度最快的伊波拉疫情。該疾病是由罕見的本迪布焦（Bundibugyo）病毒株所引起。與其他類型的伊波拉病毒不同，這種特定病毒株目前尚無核准的疫苗或治療方式，這使得當地社區面臨的處境更加危險。

paragraphs.en: Several complex factors are making it difficult for health workers to stop the virus. One major problem is the ongoing armed conflict in the eastern part of the DRC. In provinces like Ituri, North Kivu, and South Kivu, various militia groups are fighting for control of land and natural resources. Ladd Serwat resources. Ladd Serwat resources. Ladd Serwat resources. Ladd Serwat resources. Ladd Serwat resources. Ladd Serwat resources. Ladd Serwat resources. These conflicts have caused millions of people to leave their homes, creating large groups of displaced people who are harder to reach with medical care. Ladd Serwat, a senior analyst at the Acled conflict monitoring group, explained that the mix of militant activity and weak government control makes it very hard to coordinate a medical response or share important health information with the public.
paragraphs.zh: 多項複雜因素導致醫護人員難以遏止病毒傳播。其中一項主要問題是剛果民主共和國東部持續不斷的武裝衝突。在伊圖里省、北基伍省（North Kivu）與南基伍省（South Kivu）等地，各類民兵組織為爭奪土地與天然資源而戰。這些衝突導致數百萬人流離失所，形成龐大的難民群體，使得醫療照護難以觸及這些對象。衝突監測組織「武裝衝突地點與事件資料庫」（Acled）資深分析師瑟瓦特（Ladd Serwat）解釋，武裝勢力活動與政府治理能力薄弱的雙重影響，使得協調醫療應對措施或向大眾傳遞重要衛生資訊變得極為困難。

paragraphs.en: Another significant challenge is the lack of money to support health programs. Carla Martinez, the DRC head at the UN’s Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs, noted that humanitarian funding for the country dropped sharply in 2025. This was largely because the United States government stopped providing foreign aid to programs supported by the state department. As a result, more than 10 humanitarian organizations had to reduce or stop their work. Martinez explained that this weakened local health systems and surveillance networks, which are the systems used to watch for and track new illnesses. Without these networks, it is much harder to find and stop the virus before it spreads to new people.
paragraphs.zh: 另一項重大挑戰是缺乏支持衛生計畫的資金。聯合國人道事務協調廳（UN’s Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs）剛果民主共和國負責人馬丁尼茲（Carla Martinez）指出，該國的人道救援資金在二〇二五年大幅縮減。這主要是因為美國政府停止對國務院支持的計畫提供外援。結果導致超過十個人道救援組織被迫縮減或停止運作。馬丁尼茲解釋，這削弱了當地的衛生系統與監測網絡，而這些系統正是用於觀察與追蹤新興疾病的關鍵。若缺乏這些網絡，要在病毒傳播至新人群前發現並阻斷將變得更加困難。

paragraphs.en: Furthermore, health workers and their treatment centers have faced direct attacks, which has forced many to pause their life-saving work. The World Health Organization noted that the outbreak was already spreading for weeks before it was officially detected, giving the virus a head start. Because of these combined issues—conflict, a lack of funding, and attacks on medical staff—experts warn that this could become the deadliest Ebola outbreak on record.
paragraphs.zh: 此外，醫護人員及其治療中心直接遭到攻擊，迫使許多單位不得不暫停救命工作。世界衛生組織（WHO）指出，疫情在正式被偵測到之前，其實已經蔓延數週，這讓病毒取得了先機。由於衝突、資金短缺以及針對醫療人員的攻擊等問題交織，專家警告這場疫情恐將成為史上最致命的伊波拉危機。

paragraphs.en: Despite these difficult conditions, there is a small sign of hope. Researchers have recently launched a highly anticipated study to begin clinical trials for new treatments. While there is no approved cure yet, these trials represent a major step forward in the hope of fighting the virus. However, authorities are still investigating how the virus is moving between regions. For example, while one of the new suspected cases in Tshopo province was linked to the original outbreak area in Ituri, another case had no clear connection to known areas of infection. This suggests that the virus may be spreading in ways that officials do not yet fully understand.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管環境艱困，仍有一絲希望。研究人員近期啟動了一項備受期待的研究，準備針對新療法進行臨床試驗。雖然目前尚無核准的治癒方法，但這些試驗代表在對抗病毒的希望上邁出了重要的一步。然而，當局仍在調查病毒如何在不同區域間移動。例如，雖然喬波省的一起疑似病例與伊圖里省的原始疫情區有關，但另一起病例卻與已知的感染區沒有明確關聯。這顯示病毒的傳播方式可能尚未被官員完全掌握。

paragraphs.en: As the situation develops, the international community remains focused on the impact of these health challenges. The combination of a dangerous virus and a lack of resources has created a situation that is very hard to manage. For now, the priority for the Congolese government and international health groups is to improve surveillance, protect health workers, and find ways to provide care in areas affected by conflict. Whether these efforts will be enough to stop the spread remains a critical question for the region.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著情勢發展，國際社會持續關注這些衛生挑戰帶來的影響。危險病毒與資源匱乏的結合，造成了極難管理的局面。目前，剛果政府與國際衛生組織的首要任務是改善監測、保護醫護人員，並設法在受衝突影響的地區提供照護。這些努力是否足以阻止疫情擴散，仍是該地區面臨的關鍵問題。

keyTerms.definitionEn: An advantage granted or achieved at the beginning of an activity or competition.
keyTerms.noteZh: 字面上是「頭部開始」，這裡指病毒在被發現前就已經先擴散了，獲得了比防疫人員更快的進展。文中：The World Health Organization noted that the outbreak was already spreading for weeks before it was officially detected, giving the virus a head start.

keyTerms.definitionEn: The process of monitoring and tracking the spread of a disease within a population.
keyTerms.noteZh: 通常指警方或軍方的「監視、偵查」，但在公衛語境下指對疾病的持續監控。文中：Martinez explained that this weakened local health systems and surveillance networks, which are the systems used to watch for and track new illnesses.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To decrease in amount or level.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「掉落」，這裡指資金數量的減少。文中：Carla Martinez, the DRC head at the UN’s Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs, noted that humanitarian funding for the country dropped sharply in 2025.

previousArticles.title: Ebola Outbreak in Congo Spreads as Health Officials Face New Challenges
previousArticles.summaryEn: A deadly Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo has now reached 600 deaths and 1,759 confirmed cases. The virus is spreading to new provinces, including Tshopo and Haut-Uélé. Experts from the Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention describe this as the fastest-growing Ebola outbreak ever recorded. Efforts to stop the disease are being made harder by ongoing regional conflicts and a significant reduction in international aid. Researchers have recently begun clinical trials to find a treatment for this rare variant of the virus.

previousArticles.title: Michigan Parasite Outbreak Reaches Record Numbers
previousArticles.summaryEn: Michigan is experiencing its largest outbreak of cyclosporiasis in state history. Nearly 1,000 people have been infected with the parasite, which causes severe stomach illness. Health experts are working to identify the source of the contamination. While the illness is not usually fatal, it has led to dozens of hospitalizations. Investigations are ongoing across multiple states to stop the spread.

previousArticles.title: Health Officials Investigate Large Parasite Outbreak in Michigan
previousArticles.summaryEn: Michigan is currently dealing with a fast-growing outbreak of a parasite called Cyclospora. Over 700 cases have been reported to health authorities in just two weeks. While the illness is usually not deadly, it causes severe stomach problems and dehydration. Officials are working hard to find the source of the infection, which often comes from contaminated food or water. For now, experts advise people to wash all fresh produce carefully to stay safe.

quiz.question: According to the article, what is the primary reason the current Ebola outbreak is considered more dangerous than previous ones?

options.A: It has already caused more deaths than any other Ebola outbreak in history.
options.B: It is caused by a variant for which there is currently no approved vaccine or treatment.
options.C: It has spread to more countries than any other Ebola outbreak recorded.
options.D: The government data on the outbreak was not released until several months after it began.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章第二段明確指出，本次爆發是由 Bundibugyo 變異株引起的，且該變異株目前沒有核准的疫苗或治療方法，這使得情況對當地社區而言更加危險。選項 A 錯誤，因為文章僅提到專家「警告」這可能成為史上最致命的，而非目前已經是最致命的。選項 C 錯誤，文中僅提到擴散至烏干達，並未與其他歷史爆發進行跨國規模的比較。選項 D 雖然提到數據發布時間，但並非導致危險的主因。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the impact of the United States government's decision to stop providing foreign aid in 2025?

options.A: It directly caused the initial outbreak of the Bundibugyo variant in Ituri province.
options.B: It forced the Congolese government to take full control of all humanitarian organizations.
options.C: It hindered the ability of health workers to monitor and track the virus effectively.
options.D: It caused the militia groups to stop fighting for control of natural resources.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第四段，美國停止援助導致超過 10 個組織縮減工作，進而削弱了當地的醫療系統與「監測網絡」（surveillance networks），而這些網絡正是用來追蹤疾病的。因此可以推論，資金中斷直接削弱了防疫追蹤能力。選項 A 錯誤，因為疫情爆發原因與美方援助無關。選項 B 錯誤，文中並未提到政府接管組織。選項 D 錯誤，文中提到衝突持續，並未因資金減少而停止。

quiz.question: In the fifth paragraph, what does the phrase 'giving the virus a head start' mean in the context of the Ebola outbreak?

options.A: The virus was able to spread for a period of time before officials were aware of it.
options.B: The virus was moving faster than the medical staff could travel between regions.
options.C: The virus had already developed resistance to the new clinical trial treatments.
options.D: The virus was intentionally released by militia groups to gain an advantage.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。片語「a head start」原意為「領先起跑」，在文中用來形容病毒在被官方偵測到之前，已經在社區中傳播了數週，這讓病毒在防疫措施介入前就已經佔據了傳播優勢。選項 B 雖然提到速度，但不是該片語的本意。選項 C 錯誤，文中提到臨床試驗剛開始，並未提到病毒產生抗藥性。選項 D 錯誤，文中並未指控病毒是人為故意釋放的。

quiz.question: What is the central message of this article regarding the Ebola outbreak in the DRC?

options.A: The DRC government has successfully contained the virus through international funding.
options.B: Clinical trials for a new vaccine are the only way to stop the spread of the virus.
options.C: The outbreak is a complex crisis exacerbated by conflict, lack of funding, and logistical challenges.
options.D: The virus is primarily spreading because local communities refuse to accept medical help.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。本文旨在闡述剛果民主共和國面臨的嚴峻疫情，並詳細分析了導致疫情難以控制的多重因素，包括武裝衝突、資金短缺、醫療人員受襲以及監測網絡的弱化。選項 A 與事實相反，疫情尚未被控制。選項 B 過於狹隘，文中提到臨床試驗只是希望之一，並非唯一手段。選項 D 在文中並未提及，文章強調的是系統性障礙，而非民眾拒絕協助。
